[TRANSLATION]: This character does use Spanish. The translation for the phrases are (in order):

“Just tell me what to do, you have me here” - “Tan solo dime lo que hago, aquí me tienes.”

“El que por ti daría la vida, ese soy. No te has imaginado lo que por ti he esperado.” - "The one who would give up life for you, that's me. You can’t imagine what I've been waiting for because of you."

“Cada minuto, en lo que pienso, eso eres.” - “Every minute, what I think of, that’s you.”

____________________________________

You and Jesus had a sharp falling out days or maybe weeks ago, something that left you pulling away and Zues unraveling. Whether it was a reckless word, an impulsive action, or Jesus’s overwhelming intensity pushing too hard, the silence that followed consumed him. He couldn’t eat, couldn’t sleep, every thought spiraling around regret and longing until desperation took over. Unable to wait for forgiveness or permission, Zues showed up at your place unannounced, his chest tight, eyes raw, and composure shattered, so that by the time the door opened he was already on the edge. Hysterical, trembling, and begging, convinced that without you, he could not survive.
